NKJV.1: Then Jesus went out and departed from the temple, and His disciples came up to show Him the buildings of the temple.
NKJV.2: And Jesus said to them, “Do you not see all these things? Assuredly, I say to you, not one stone shall be left here upon another, that shall not be thrown down.”
NKJV.3: Now as He sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to Him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will these things be? And what will be the sign of Your coming, and of the end of the age?”
NKJV.4: And Jesus answered and said to them: “Take heed that no one deceives you.
NKJV.5: For  many will come in My name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’  and will deceive many.
NKJV.6: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not troubled; for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
NKJV.7: For  nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. And there will be  famines, pestilences, and earthquakes in various places.
NKJV.8: All these are the beginning of sorrows.
NKJV.9: “Then they will deliver you up to tribulation and kill you, and you will be hated by all nations for My name’s sake.
NKJV.10: And then many will be offended, will betray one another, and will hate one another.
NKJV.11: Then  many false prophets will rise up and  deceive many.
NKJV.12: And because lawlessness will abound, the love of many will grow  cold.
NKJV.13: But he who endures to the end shall be saved.
NKJV.14: And this  gospel of the kingdom  will be preached in all the world as a witness to all the nations, and then the end will come.
NKJV.15: “Therefore when you see the  ‘abomination of desolation,’ spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place” (whoever reads, let him understand),
NKJV.16: “then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
NKJV.17: Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take anything out of his house.
NKJV.18: And let him who is in the field not go back to get his clothes.
NKJV.19: But  woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
NKJV.20: And pray that your flight may not be in winter or on the Sabbath.
NKJV.21: For  then there will be great tribulation, such as has not been since the beginning of the world until this time, no, nor ever shall be.
NKJV.22: And unless those days were shortened, no flesh would be saved;  but for the elect’s sake those days will be shortened.
NKJV.23: “Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘There!’ do not believe it.
NKJV.24: For  false christs and false prophets will rise and show great signs and wonders to deceive,  if possible, even the elect.
NKJV.25: See, I have told you beforehand.
NKJV.26: “Therefore if they say to you, ‘Look, He is in the desert!’ do not go out; or ‘Look, He is in the inner rooms!’ do not believe it.
NKJV.27: For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes to the west, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
NKJV.28: For wherever the carcass is, there the eagles will be gathered together.
NKJV.29: “Immediately after the tribulation of those days  the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
NKJV.30: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven,  and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
NKJV.31: And He will send His angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they will gather together His elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
NKJV.32: “Now learn this parable from the fig tree: When its branch has already become tender and puts forth leaves, you know that summer is near.
NKJV.33: So you also, when you see all these things, know  that it is near—at the doors!
NKJV.34: Assuredly, I say to you,  this generation will by no means pass away till all these things take place.
NKJV.35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will by no means pass away.
NKJV.36: “But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, but My Father only.
NKJV.37: But as the days of Noah were, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
NKJV.38: For as in the days before the flood, they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark,
NKJV.39: and did not know until the flood came and took them all away, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
NKJV.40: Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left.
NKJV.41: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.
NKJV.42: Watch therefore, for you do not know what hour your Lord is coming.
NKJV.43: But know this, that if the master of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched and not allowed his house to be broken into.
NKJV.44: Therefore you also be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.
NKJV.45: “Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his master made ruler over his household, to give them food in due season?
NKJV.46: Blessed is that servant whom his master, when he comes, will find so doing.
NKJV.47: Assuredly, I say to you that  he will make him ruler over all his goods.
NKJV.48: But if that evil servant says in his heart, ‘My master  is delaying his coming,’
NKJV.49: and begins to beat his fellow servants, and to eat and drink with the drunkards,
NKJV.50: the master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him and at an hour that he is not aware of,

ESV.1: Jesus left the temple and was going away, when his disciples came to point out to him the buildings of the temple.
ESV.2: But he answered them, “You see all these, do you not? Truly, I say to you, there will not be left here one stone upon another that will not be thrown down.”
ESV.3: As he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
ESV.4: And Jesus answered them,  “See that no one leads you astray.
ESV.5: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’ and they will lead many astray.
ESV.6: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not alarmed, for this must take place, but the end is not yet.
ESV.7: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
ESV.8: All these are but the beginning of the birth pains.
ESV.9: “Then they will deliver you up to tribulation and put you to death, and you will be hated by all nations for my name's sake.
ESV.10: And then many will fall away  and betray one another and hate one another.
ESV.11: And many false prophets will arise and lead many astray.
ESV.12: And because lawlessness will be increased, the love of many will grow cold.
ESV.13: But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
ESV.14: And this gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
ESV.15: “So when you see the abomination of desolation spoken of by the prophet Daniel, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
ESV.16: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
ESV.17: Let the one who is on the housetop not go down to take what is in his house,
ESV.18: and let the one who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak.
ESV.19: And alas for women who are pregnant and for those who are nursing infants in those days!
ESV.20: Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a Sabbath.
ESV.21: For then there will be great tribulation, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, and never will be.
ESV.22: And if those days had not been cut short, no human being would be saved. But for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short.
ESV.23: Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘There he is!’ do not believe it.
ESV.24: For false christs and false prophets will arise and perform great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
ESV.25: See, I have told you beforehand.
ESV.26: So, if they say to you, ‘Look, he is in the wilderness,’ do not go out. If they say, ‘Look, he is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.
ESV.27: For as the lightning comes from the east and shines as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
ESV.28: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
ESV.29: “Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light, and the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
ESV.30: Then will appear in heaven the sign of the Son of Man, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
ESV.31: And he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
ESV.32: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
ESV.33: So also, when you see all these things, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
ESV.34: Truly, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
ESV.35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
ESV.36: “But concerning that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, nor the Son,  but the Father only.
ESV.37: For as were the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
ESV.38: For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day when Noah entered the ark,
ESV.39: and they were unaware until the flood came and swept them all away, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
ESV.40: Then two men will be in the field; one will be taken and one left.
ESV.41: Two women will be grinding at the mill; one will be taken and one left.
ESV.42: Therefore, stay awake, for you do not know on what day your Lord is coming.
ESV.43: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what part of the night the thief was coming, he would have stayed awake and would not have let his house be broken into.
ESV.44: Therefore you also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.
ESV.45: “Who then is the faithful and wise servant,  whom his master has set over his household, to give them their food at the proper time?
ESV.46: Blessed is that servant whom his master will find so doing when he comes.
ESV.47: Truly, I say to you, he will set him over all his possessions.
ESV.48: But if that wicked servant says to himself, ‘My master is delayed,’
ESV.49: and begins to beat his fellow servants  and eats and drinks with drunkards,
ESV.50: the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know
ESV.51: and will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

NASB.1: Jesus came out from the temple and was going away when His disciples came up to point out the temple buildings to Him.
NASB.2: And He said to them, 'Do you not see all these things? Truly I say to you, not one stone here will be left upon another, which will not be torn down.'
NASB.3: As He was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to Him privately, saying, 'Tell us, when will these things happen, and what [will be] the sign of Your coming, and of the end of the age?'
NASB.4: And Jesus answered and said to them, 'See to it that no one misleads you.
NASB.5: 'For many will come in My name, saying, 'I am the Christ,' and will mislead many.
NASB.6: 'You will be hearing of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not frightened, for [those things] must take place, but [that] is not yet the end.
NASB.7: 'For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and in various places there will be famines and earthquakes.
NASB.8: 'But all these things are [merely] the beginning of birth pangs.
NASB.9: 'Then they will deliver you to tribulation, and will kill you, and you will be hated by all nations because of My name.
NASB.10: 'At that time many will fall away and will betray one another and hate one another.
NASB.11: 'Many false prophets will arise and will mislead many.
NASB.12: 'Because lawlessness is increased, most people's love will grow cold.
NASB.13: 'But the one who endures to the end, he will be saved.
NASB.14: 'This gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in the whole world as a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come.
NASB.15: 'Therefore when you see the ABOMINATION OF DESOLATION which was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
NASB.16: then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains.
NASB.17: 'Whoever is on the housetop must not go down to get the things out that are in his house.
NASB.18: 'Whoever is in the field must not turn back to get his cloak.
NASB.19: 'But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
NASB.20: 'But pray that your flight will not be in the winter, or on a Sabbath.
NASB.21: 'For then there will be a great tribulation, such as has not occurred since the beginning of the world until now, nor ever will.
NASB.22: 'Unless those days had been cut short, no life would have been saved; but for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short.
NASB.23: 'Then if anyone says to you, 'Behold, here is the Christ,' or 'There [He is],' do not believe [him].
NASB.24: 'For false Christs and false prophets will arise and will show great signs and wonders, so as to mislead, if possible, even the elect.
NASB.25: 'Behold, I have told you in advance.
NASB.26: 'So if they say to you, 'Behold, He is in the wilderness,' do not go out, [or], 'Behold, He is in the inner rooms,' do not believe [them].
NASB.27: 'For just as the lightning comes from the east and flashes even to the west, so will the coming of the Son of Man be.
NASB.28: 'Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
NASB.29: 'But immediately after the tribulation of those days THE SUN WILL BE DARKENED, AND THE MOON WILL NOT GIVE ITS LIGHT, AND THE STARS WILL FALL from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
NASB.30: 'And then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the SON OF MAN COMING ON THE CLOUDS OF THE SKY with power and great glory.
NASB.31: 'And He will send forth His angels with A GREAT TRUMPET and THEY WILL GATHER TOGETHER His elect from the four winds, from one end of the sky to the other.
NASB.32: 'Now learn the parable from the fig tree: when its branch has already become tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near;
NASB.33: so, you too, when you see all these things, recognize that He is near, [right] at the door.
NASB.34: 'Truly I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
NASB.35: 'Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
NASB.36: 'But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, nor the Son, but the Father alone.
NASB.37: 'For the coming of the Son of Man will be just like the days of Noah.
NASB.38: 'For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark,
NASB.39: and they did not understand until the flood came and took them all away; so will the coming of the Son of Man be.
NASB.40: 'Then there will be two men in the field; one will be taken and one will be left.
NASB.41: 'Two women [will be] grinding at the mill; one will be taken and one will be left.
NASB.42: 'Therefore be on the alert, for you do not know which day your Lord is coming.
NASB.43: 'But be sure of this, that if the head of the house had known at what time of the night the thief was coming, he would have been on the alert and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
NASB.44: 'For this reason you also must be ready; for the Son of Man is coming at an hour when you do not think [He will].
NASB.45: 'Who then is the faithful and sensible slave whom his master put in charge of his household to give them their food at the proper time?
NASB.46: 'Blessed is that slave whom his master finds so doing when he comes.
NASB.47: 'Truly I say to you that he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
NASB.48: 'But if that evil slave says in his heart, 'My master is not coming for a long time,'
NASB.49: and begins to beat his fellow slaves and eat and drink with drunkards;
NASB.50: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect [him] and at an hour which he does not know,
NASB.51: and will cut him in pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

NLT.1: As Jesus was leaving the Temple grounds, his disciples pointed out to him the various Temple buildings.
NLT.2: But he responded, “Do you see all these buildings? I tell you the truth, they will be completely demolished. Not one stone will be left on top of another!”
NLT.3: Later, Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives. His disciples came to him privately and said, “Tell us, when will all this happen? What sign will signal your return and the end of the world? ”
NLT.4: Jesus told them, “Don’t let anyone mislead you,
NLT.5: for many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am the Messiah.’ They will deceive many.
NLT.6: And you will hear of wars and threats of wars, but don’t panic. Yes, these things must take place, but the end won’t follow immediately.
NLT.7: Nation will go to war against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines and earthquakes in many parts of the world.
NLT.8: But all this is only the first of the birth pains, with more to come.
NLT.9: “Then you will be arrested, persecuted, and killed. You will be hated all over the world because you are my followers.
NLT.10: And many will turn away from me and betray and hate each other.
NLT.11: And many false prophets will appear and will deceive many people.
NLT.12: Sin will be rampant everywhere, and the love of many will grow cold.
NLT.13: But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
NLT.14: And the Good News about the Kingdom will be preached throughout the whole world, so that all nations will hear it; and then the end will come.
NLT.15: “The day is coming when you will see what Daniel the prophet spoke about — the sacrilegious object that causes desecration standing in the Holy Place.” (Reader, pay attention!)
NLT.16: “Then those in Judea must flee to the hills.
NLT.17: A person out on the deck of a roof must not go down into the house to pack.
NLT.18: A person out in the field must not return even to get a coat.
NLT.19: How terrible it will be for pregnant women and for nursing mothers in those days.
NLT.20: And pray that your flight will not be in winter or on the Sabbath.
NLT.21: For there will be greater anguish than at any time since the world began. And it will never be so great again.
NLT.22: In fact, unless that time of calamity is shortened, not a single person will survive. But it will be shortened for the sake of God’s chosen ones.
NLT.23: “Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Messiah,’ or ‘There he is,’ don’t believe it.
NLT.24: For false messiahs and false prophets will rise up and perform great signs and wonders so as to deceive, if possible, even God’s chosen ones.
NLT.25: See, I have warned you about this ahead of time.
NLT.26: “So if someone tells you, ‘Look, the Messiah is out in the desert,’ don’t bother to go and look. Or, ‘Look, he is hiding here,’ don’t believe it!
NLT.27: For as the lightning flashes in the east and shines to the west, so it will be when the Son of Man comes.
NLT.28: Just as the gathering of vultures shows there is a carcass nearby, so these signs indicate that the end is near.
NLT.29:
“Immediately after the anguish of those days, the sun will be darkened,
the moon will give no light,
the stars will fall from the sky,
and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
NLT.30: And then at last, the sign that the Son of Man is coming will appear in the heavens, and there will be deep mourning among all the peoples of the earth. And they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
NLT.31: And he will send out his angels with the mighty blast of a trumpet, and they will gather his chosen ones from all over the world — from the farthest ends of the earth and heaven.
NLT.32: “Now learn a lesson from the fig tree. When its branches bud and its leaves begin to sprout, you know that summer is near.
NLT.33: In the same way, when you see all these things, you can know his return is very near, right at the door.
NLT.34: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass from the scene until all these things take place.
NLT.35: Heaven and earth will disappear, but my words will never disappear.
NLT.36: “However, no one knows the day or hour when these things will happen, not even the angels in heaven or the Son himself. Only the Father knows.
NLT.37: “When the Son of Man returns, it will be like it was in Noah’s day.
NLT.38: In those days before the flood, the people were enjoying banquets and parties and weddings right up to the time Noah entered his boat.
NLT.39: People didn’t realize what was going to happen until the flood came and swept them all away. That is the way it will be when the Son of Man comes.
NLT.40: “Two men will be working together in the field; one will be taken, the other left.
NLT.41: Two women will be grinding flour at the mill; one will be taken, the other left.
NLT.42: “So you, too, must keep watch! For you don’t know what day your Lord is coming.
NLT.43: Understand this: If a homeowner knew exactly when a burglar was coming, he would keep watch and not permit his house to be broken into.
NLT.44: You also must be ready all the time, for the Son of Man will come when least expected.
NLT.45: “A faithful, sensible servant is one to whom the master can give the responsibility of managing his other household servants and feeding them.
NLT.46: If the master returns and finds that the servant has done a good job, there will be a reward.
NLT.47: I tell you the truth, the master will put that servant in charge of all he owns.
NLT.48: But what if the servant is evil and thinks, ‘My master won’t be back for a while,’
NLT.49: and he begins beating the other servants, partying, and getting drunk?
NLT.50: The master will return unannounced and unexpected,
NLT.51: and he will cut the servant to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

WEB.1: Jesus went out from the temple, and was going on his way. His disciples came to him to show him the buildings of the temple.
WEB.2: But he answered them, “You see all of these things, don’t you? Most certainly I tell you, there will not be left here one stone on another, that will not be thrown down.”
WEB.3: As he sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign of your coming, and of the end of the age?”
WEB.4: Jesus answered them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.
WEB.5: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’ and will lead many astray.
WEB.6: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you aren’t troubled, for all this must happen, but the end is not yet.
WEB.7: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there will be famines, plagues, and earthquakes in various places.
WEB.8: But all these things are the beginning of birth pains.
WEB.9: Then they will deliver you up to oppression, and will kill you. You will be hated by all of the nations for my name’s sake.
WEB.10: Then many will stumble, and will deliver up one another, and will hate one another.
WEB.11: Many false prophets will arise, and will lead many astray.
WEB.12: Because iniquity will be multiplied, the love of many will grow cold.
WEB.13: But he who endures to the end, the same will be saved.
WEB.14: This Good News of the Kingdom will be preached in the whole world for a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come.
WEB.15: “When, therefore, you see the abomination of desolation,  which was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
WEB.16: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
WEB.17: Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take out the things that are in his house.
WEB.18: Let him who is in the field not return back to get his clothes.
WEB.19: But woe to those who are with child and to nursing mothers in those days!
WEB.20: Pray that your flight will not be in the winter, nor on a Sabbath,
WEB.21: for then there will be great oppression, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, nor ever will be.
WEB.22: Unless those days had been shortened, no flesh would have been saved. But for the sake of the chosen ones, those days will be shortened.
WEB.23: “Then if any man tells you, ‘Behold, here is the Christ,’ or, ‘There,’ don’t believe it.
WEB.24: For there will arise false christs, and false prophets, and they will show great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.
WEB.26: If therefore they tell you, ‘Behold, he is in the wilderness,’ don’t go out; ‘Behold, he is in the inner rooms,’ don’t believe it.
WEB.27: For as the lightning flashes from the east, and is seen even to the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
WEB.28: For wherever the carcass is, there is where the vultures  gather together.
WEB.29: But immediately after the oppression of those days, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light, the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken;
WEB.30: and then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky. Then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of the sky with power and great glory.
WEB.31: He will send out his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from one end of the sky to the other.
WEB.32: “Now from the fig tree learn this parable. When its branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near.
WEB.33: Even so you also, when you see all these things, know that it is near, even at the doors.
WEB.34: Most certainly I tell you, this generation  will not pass away, until all these things are accomplished.
WEB.35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
WEB.36: But no one knows of that day and hour, not even the angels of heaven,  but my Father only.
WEB.37: “As the days of Noah were, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
WEB.38: For as in those days which were before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ship,
WEB.39: and they didn’t know until the flood came, and took them all away, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
WEB.40: Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and one will be left;
WEB.41: two women grinding at the mill, one will be taken and one will be left.
WEB.42: Watch therefore, for you don’t know in what hour your Lord comes.
WEB.43: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what watch of the night the thief was coming, he would have watched, and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
WEB.44: Therefore also be ready, for in an hour that you don’t expect, the Son of Man will come.
WEB.45: “Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his lord has set over his household, to give them their food in due season?
WEB.46: Blessed is that servant whom his lord finds doing so when he comes.
WEB.47: Most certainly I tell you that he will set him over all that he has.
WEB.48: But if that evil servant should say in his heart, ‘My lord is delaying his coming,’
WEB.49: and begins to beat his fellow servants, and eat and drink with the drunkards,
WEB.50: the lord of that servant will come in a day when he doesn’t expect it, and in an hour when he doesn’t know it,
WEB.51: and will cut him in pieces, and appoint his portion with the hypocrites. There is where the weeping and grinding of teeth will be.

CSB17.12: Because lawlessness <sup>ⓐ</sup> will multiply, the love of many will grow cold.
CSB17.13: But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
CSB17.14: This good news of the kingdom will be proclaimed in all the world <sup>[2]</sup> as a testimony to all nations, <sup>ⓑ</sup> and then the end will come. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.15: “So when you see the abomination of desolation, [3] spoken of by the prophet Daniel, ⓓ standing in the holy place” ⓔ (let the reader understand),
CSB17.16: “then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
CSB17.17: A man on the housetop <sup>[4]</sup> must not come down to get things out of his house, <sup>ⓕ</sup>

AMP.1: Jesus left the temple area and was going on His way when His disciples came up to Him to call His attention to the [magnificent and massive] buildings of the temple.
AMP.2: And He said to them, "Do you see all these things? I assure you and most solemnly say to you, not one stone here will be left on another, which will not be torn down."
AMP.3: While Jesus was seated on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to Him privately, and said, "Tell us, when will this [destruction of the temple] take place, and what will be the sign of Your coming, and of the end (completion, consummation) of the age?"
AMP.4: Jesus answered, "Be careful that no one misleads you [deceiving you and leading you into error].
AMP.5: For many will come in My name [misusing it, and appropriating the strength of the name which belongs to Me], saying, 'I am the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed),' and they will mislead many.
AMP.6: You will continually hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not frightened, for those things must take place, but that is not yet the end [of the age].
AMP.7: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
AMP.8: But all these things are merely the beginning of birth pangs [of the intolerable anguish and the time of unprecedented trouble].
AMP.9: "Then they will hand you over to [endure] tribulation, and will put you to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of My name.
AMP.10: At that time many will be offended and repelled [by their association with Me] and will fall away [from the One whom they should trust] and will betray one another [handing over believers to their persecutors] and will hate one another.
AMP.11: Many false prophets will appear and mislead many.
AMP.12: Because lawlessness is increased, the love of most people will grow cold.
AMP.13: But the one who endures and bears up [under suffering] to the end will be saved.
AMP.14: This good news of the kingdom [the gospel] will be preached throughout the whole world as a testimony to all the nations, and then the end [of the age] will come.
AMP.15: "So when you see theabomination of desolation [the appalling sacrilege that astonishes and makes desolate], spoken of by the prophet Daniel, standing in the Holy Place (let the reader understand),
AMP.16: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains [for refuge].
AMP.17: "Whoever is on the housetop must not go down to get the things that are in his house [because there will not be enough time].
AMP.18: "Whoever is in the field must not turn back to get his coat.
AMP.19: And woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
AMP.20: Pray that your flight [from persecution and suffering] will not be in winter, or on a Sabbath [when Jewish laws prohibit travel].
AMP.21: For at that time there will be a great tribulation (pressure, distress, oppression), such as has not occurred since the beginning of the world until now, nor ever will [again].
AMP.22: And if those days [of tribulation] had not been cut short, no human life would be saved; but for the sake of the elect (God's chosen ones) those days will be shortened.
AMP.23: Then if anyone says to you [during the great tribulation], 'Look! Here is the Christ,' or 'There He is,' do not believe it.
AMP.24: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and they will provide great signs and wonders, so as to deceive, if possible, even the elect (God's chosen ones).
AMP.25: Listen carefully, I have told you in advance.
AMP.26: So if they say to you, 'Look! He is in the wilderness,' do not go out there, or, 'Look! He is in the inner rooms [of a house],' do not believe it.
AMP.27: For just as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west, so will be the coming [in glory] of the Son of Man [everyone will see Him clearly].
AMP.28: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will flock together.
AMP.29: "Immediately after the tribulation of those days <e>the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not provide its light, and the stars will fall</e> from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
AMP.30: And at that time the sign of the Son of Man [coming in His glory] will appear in the sky, and then all the tribes of the earth [and especially Israel] will mourn [regretting their rebellion and rejection of the Messiah], and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory [in brilliance and splendor].
AMP.31: And He will send His angels with <e>a loud trumpet</e> and <e>they will gather together</e> His elect (God's chosen ones) from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other.
AMP.32: "Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its young shoots become tender and it puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near;
AMP.33: so you, too, when you see all these things [taking place], know for certain that He is near, right at the door.
AMP.34: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, this generation [the people living when these signs and events begin] will not pass away until all these things take place.
AMP.35: Heaven and earth [as now known] will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
AMP.36: "But of that [exact] day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, nor the Son [in His humanity], but the Father alone.
AMP.37: For the coming of the Son of Man (the Messiah) will be just like the days of Noah.
AMP.38: For as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the [very] day when Noah entered the ark,
AMP.39: and they did not know or understand until the flood came and swept them all away; so will the coming of the Son of Man be [unexpected judgment].
AMP.40: At that time two men will be in the field; one will be taken [for judgment] and one will be left.
AMP.41: Two women will be grinding at the mill; one will be taken [for judgment] and one will be left.
AMP.42: "So be alert [give strict attention, be cautious and active in faith], for you do not know which day [whether near or far] your Lord is coming.
AMP.43: But understand this: If the head of the house had known what time of the night the thief was coming, he would have been on the alert and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.
AMP.44: Therefore, you [who follow Me] must also be ready; because the Son of Man is coming at an hour when you do not expect Him.
AMP.45: "Who then is the faithful and wise servant whom his master has put in charge of his household to give the others [in the house] their food and supplies at the proper time?
AMP.46: Blessed is that [faithful] servant when his master returns and finds him doing so.
AMP.47: I assure you and most solemnly say to you that he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
AMP.48: But if that servant is evil and says in his heart, 'My master is taking his time [he will not return for a long while],'
AMP.49: and begins to beat his fellow servants and to eat and drink with drunkards;
AMP.50: the master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour of which he is not aware,
AMP.51: and will cut him in two and put him with the hypocrites; in that place there will be weeping [over sorrow and pain] and grinding of teeth [over distress and anger].

MSG.1: Jesus then left the Temple. As he walked away, his disciples pointed out how very impressive the Temple architecture was.
MSG.2: Jesus said, "You're not impressed by all this sheer size, are you? The truth of the matter is that there's not a stone in that building that is not going to end up in a pile of rubble."
MSG.3: Later as he was sitting on Mount Olives, his disciples approached and asked him, "Tell us, when are these things going to happen? What will be the sign of your coming, that the time's up?"
MSG.4: Jesus said, "Watch out for doomsday deceivers.
MSG.5: Many leaders are going to show up with forged identities, claiming, 'I am Christ, the Messiah.' They will deceive a lot of people.
MSG.6: When reports come in of wars and rumored wars, keep your head and don't panic. This is routine history; this is no sign of the end.
MSG.7: Nation will fight nation and ruler fight ruler, over and over. Famines and earthquakes will occur in various places.
MSG.8: This is nothing compared to what is coming.
MSG.9: "They are going to throw you to the wolves and kill you, everyone hating you because you carry my name.
MSG.10: And then, going from bad to worse, it will be dog-eat-dog, everyone at each other's throat, everyone hating each other.
MSG.11: "In the confusion, lying preachers will come forward and deceive a lot of people.
MSG.12: For many others, the overwhelming spread of evil will do them in—nothing left of their love but a mound of ashes.
MSG.13: "Staying with it—that's what God requires. Stay with it to the end. You won't be sorry, and you'll be saved.
MSG.14: All during this time, the good news—the Message of the kingdom—will be preached all over the world, a witness staked out in every country. And then the end will come.
MSG.15: "But be ready to run for it when you see the monster of desecration set up in the Temple sanctuary. The prophet Daniel described this. If you've read Daniel, you'll know what I'm talking about.
MSG.16: If you're living in Judea at the time, run for the hills;
MSG.17: if you're working in the yard, don't return to the house to get anything;
MSG.18: if you're out in the field, don't go back and get your coat.
MSG.19: Pregnant and nursing mothers will have it especially hard.
MSG.20: Hope and pray this won't happen during the winter or on a Sabbath.
MSG.21: "This is going to be trouble on a scale beyond what the world has ever seen, or will see again.
MSG.22: If these days of trouble were left to run their course, nobody would make it. But on account of God's chosen people, the trouble will be cut short.
MSG.23: "If anyone tries to flag you down, calling out, 'Here's the Messiah!' or points, 'There he is!' don't fall for it.
MSG.24: Fake Messiahs and lying preachers are going to pop up everywhere. Their impressive credentials and dazzling performances will pull the wool over the eyes of even those who ought to know better.
MSG.25: But I've given you fair warning.
MSG.26: "So if they say, 'Run to the country and see him arrive!' or, 'Quick, get downtown, see him come!' don't give them the time of day.
MSG.27: The Arrival of the Son of Man isn't something you go to see. He comes like swift lightning to you!
MSG.28: Whenever you see crowds gathering, think of carrion vultures circling, moving in, hovering over a rotting carcass. You can be quite sure that it's not the living Son of Man pulling in those crowds.
MSG.29: "Following those hard times, Sun will fade out, moon cloud over, Stars fall out of the sky, cosmic powers tremble.
MSG.30: "Then, the Arrival of the Son of Man! It will fill the skies—no one will miss it. Unready people all over the world, outsiders to the splendor and power, will raise a huge lament as they watch the Son of Man blazing out of heaven.
MSG.31: At that same moment, he'll dispatch his angels with a trumpet-blast summons, pulling in God's chosen from the four winds, from pole to pole.
MSG.32: "Take a lesson from the fig tree. From the moment you notice its buds form, the merest hint of green, you know summer's just around the corner.
MSG.33: So it is with you: When you see all these things, you'll know he's at the door.
MSG.34: Don't take this lightly. I'm not just saying this for some future generation, but for all of you. This age continues until all these things take place.
MSG.35: Sky and earth will wear out; my words won't wear out.
MSG.36: "But the exact day and hour? No one knows that, not even heaven's angels, not even the Son. Only the Father knows.
MSG.37: "The Arrival of the Son of Man will take place in times like Noah's.
MSG.38: Before the great flood everyone was carrying on as usual, having a good time right up to the day Noah boarded the ark.
MSG.39: They knew nothing—until the flood hit and swept everything away. "The Son of Man's Arrival will be like that:
MSG.40: Two men will be working in the field—one will be taken, one left behind;
MSG.41: two women will be grinding at the mill—one will be taken, one left behind.
MSG.42: So stay awake, alert. You have no idea what day your Master will show up.
MSG.43: But you do know this: You know that if the homeowner had known what time of night the burglar would arrive, he would have been there with his dogs to prevent the break-in.
MSG.44: Be vigilant just like that. You have no idea when the Son of Man is going to show up.
MSG.45: "Who here qualifies for the job of overseeing the kitchen? A person the Master can depend on to feed the workers on time each day.
MSG.46: Someone the Master can drop in on unannounced and always find him doing his job. A God-blessed man or woman, I tell you.
MSG.47: It won't be long before the Master will put this person in charge of the whole operation.
MSG.48: "But if that person only looks out for himself, and the minute the Master is away does what he pleases—
MSG.49: abusing the help and throwing drunken parties for his friends—
MSG.50: the Master is going to show up when he least expects it
MSG.51: and make hash of him. He'll end up in the dump with the hypocrites, out in the cold shivering, teeth chattering.

NIV.1:
Signs of the End of the Age
Jesus left the temple and was walking away when his disciples came up to him to call his attention to its buildings.
NIV.2: “Do you see all these things?” he asked. “I tell you the truth, not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.”
NIV.3: As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to him privately. “Tell us,” they said, “when will this happen, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
NIV.4: Jesus answered: “Watch out that no one deceives you.
NIV.5: For many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am the Christ,’ and will deceive many.
NIV.6: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars, but see to it that you are not alarmed. Such things must happen, but the end is still to come.
NIV.7: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
NIV.8: All these are the beginning of birth pains.
NIV.9: “Then you will be handed over to be persecuted and put to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of me.
NIV.10: At that time many will turn away from the faith and will betray and hate each other,
NIV.11: and many false prophets will appear and deceive many people.
NIV.12: Because of the increase of wickedness, the love of most will grow cold,
NIV.13: but he who stands firm to the end will be saved.
NIV.14: And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached in the whole world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
NIV.15: “So when you see standing in the holy place ‘the abomination that causes desolation,’ spoken of through the prophet Daniel—let the reader understand—
NIV.16: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
NIV.17: Let no one on the roof of his house go down to take anything out of the house.
NIV.18: Let no one in the field go back to get his cloak.
NIV.19: How dreadful it will be in those days for pregnant women and nursing mothers!
NIV.20: Pray that your flight will not take place in winter or on the Sabbath.
NIV.21: For then there will be great distress, unequaled from the beginning of the world until now—and never to be equaled again.
NIV.22: If those days had not been cut short, no one would survive, but for the sake of the elect those days will be shortened.
NIV.23: At that time if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘There he is!’ do not believe it.
NIV.24: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform great signs and miracles to deceive even the elect—if that were possible.
NIV.25: See, I have told you ahead of time.
NIV.26: “So if anyone tells you, ‘There he is, out in the desert,’ do not go out; or, ‘Here he is, in the inner rooms,’ do not believe it.
NIV.27: For as lightning that comes from the east is visible even in the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
NIV.28: Wherever there is a carcass, there the vultures will gather.
NIV.29:
“Immediately after the distress of those days
“‘the sun will be darkened,
and the moon will not give its light;
the stars will fall from the sky,
and the heavenly bodies will be shaken.’
NIV.30: “At that time the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky, and all the nations of the earth will mourn. They will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of the sky, with power and great glory.
NIV.31: And he will send his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of the heavens to the other.
NIV.32: “Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its twigs get tender and its leaves come out, you know that summer is near.
NIV.33: Even so, when you see all these things, you know that it is near, right at the door.
NIV.34: I tell you the truth, this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things have happened.
NIV.35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
NIV.36:
The Day and Hour Unknown
“No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
NIV.37: As it was in the days of Noah, so it will be at the coming of the Son of Man.
NIV.38: For in the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark;
NIV.39: and they knew nothing about what would happen until the flood came and took them all away. That is how it will be at the coming of the Son of Man.
NIV.40: Two men will be in the field; one will be taken and the other left.
NIV.41: Two women will be grinding with a hand mill; one will be taken and the other left.
NIV.42: “Therefore keep watch, because you do not know on what day your Lord will come.
NIV.43: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what time of night the thief was coming, he would have kept watch and would not have let his house be broken into.
NIV.44: So you also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.
NIV.45: “Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom the master has put in charge of the servants in his household to give them their food at the proper time?
NIV.46: It will be good for that servant whose master finds him doing so when he returns.
NIV.47: I tell you the truth, he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
NIV.48: But suppose that servant is wicked and says to himself, ‘My master is staying away a long time,’
NIV.49: and he then begins to beat his fellow servants and to eat and drink with drunkards.
NIV.50: The master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he is not aware of.
NIV.51: He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

NRSVCE.46: Blessed is that slave whom his master will find at work when he arrives.
NRSVCE.47: Truly I tell you, he will put that one in charge of all his possessions.
NRSVCE.48: But if that wicked slave says to himself, ‘My master is delayed,’
NRSVCE.49: and he begins to beat his fellow slaves, and eats and drinks with drunkards,
NRSVCE.50: the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour that he does not know.
NRSVCE.51: He will cut him in pieces and put him with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

NET.1: Now as Jesus was going out of the temple courts and walking away, his disciples came to show him the temple buildings.
NET.2: And he said to them, “Do you see all these things? I tell you the truth, not one stone will be left on another. All will be torn down!”
NET.3: As he was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him privately and said, “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?”
NET.4: Jesus answered them, “Watch out that no one misleads you.
NET.5: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am the Christ,’ and they will mislead many.
NET.6: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars. Make sure that you are not alarmed, for this must happen, but the end is still to come.
NET.7: For nation will rise up in arms against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. And there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
NET.8: All these things are the beginning of birth pains.
NET.9: “Then they will hand you over to be persecuted and will kill you. You will be hated by all the nations because of my name.
NET.10: Then many will be led into sin, and they will betray one another and hate one another.
NET.11: And many false prophets will appear and deceive many,
NET.12: and because lawlessness will increase so much, the love of many will grow cold.
NET.13: But the person who endures to the end will be saved.
NET.14: And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached throughout the whole inhabited earth as a testimony to all the nations, and then the end will come.
NET.15: “So when you see the abomination of desolation — spoken about by Daniel the prophet — standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
NET.16: then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
NET.17: The one on the roof must not come down to take anything out of his house,
NET.18: and the one in the field must not turn back to get his cloak.
NET.19: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing their babies in those days!
NET.20: Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a Sabbath.
NET.21: For then there will be great suffering unlike anything that has happened from the beginning of the world until now, or ever will happen.
NET.22: And if those days had not been cut short, no one would be saved. But for the sake of the elect those days will be cut short.
NET.23: Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘There he is!’ do not believe him.
NET.24: For false messiahs and false prophets will appear and perform great signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect.
NET.25: Remember, I have told you ahead of time.
NET.26: So then, if someone says to you, ‘Look, he is in the wilderness,’ do not go out, or ‘Look, he is in the inner rooms,’ do not believe him.
NET.27: For just like the lightning comes from the east and flashes to the west, so the coming of the Son of Man will be.
NET.28: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather.
NET.29: “Immediately after the suffering of those days, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of heaven will be shaken.
NET.30: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and all the tribes of the earth will mourn. They will see the Son of Man arriving on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
NET.31: And he will send his angels with a loud trumpet blast, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
NET.32: “Learn this parable from the fig tree: Whenever its branch becomes tender and puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
NET.33: So also you, when you see all these things, know that he is near, right at the door.
NET.34: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
NET.35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
NET.36: “But as for that day and hour no one knows it — not even the angels in heaven — except the Father alone.
NET.37: For just like the days of Noah were, so the coming of the Son of Man will be.
NET.38: For in those days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day Noah entered the ark.
NET.39: And they knew nothing until the flood came and took them all away. It will be the same at the coming of the Son of Man.
NET.40: Then there will be two men in the field; one will be taken and one left.
NET.41: There will be two women grinding grain with a mill; one will be taken and one left.
NET.42: “Therefore stay alert, because you do not know on what day your Lord will come.
NET.43: But understand this: If the owner of the house had known at what time of night the thief was coming, he would have been alert and would not have let his house be broken into.
NET.44: Therefore you also must be ready, because the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.
NET.45: “Who then is the faithful and wise slave, whom the master has put in charge of his household, to give the other slaves their food at the proper time?
NET.46: Blessed is that slave whom the master finds at work when he comes.
NET.47: I tell you the truth, the master will put him in charge of all his possessions.
NET.48: But if that evil slave should say to himself, ‘My master is staying away a long time,’
NET.49: and he begins to beat his fellow slaves and to eat and drink with drunkards,
NET.50: then the master of that slave will come on a day when he does not expect him and at an hour he does not foresee,
NET.51: and will cut him in two, and assign him a place with the hypocrites, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
